mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.5, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding consumer credit balances indicate synchronized strength in durable goods demand and household balance sheets, which historically precedes broad equity outperformance over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in 60-day forward returns across two consecutive economic cycles, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index exceeding 100 signals robust manufacturing capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, which expands profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month forward window.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain flat or negative when INDPRO sustains values above 100 for three consecutive months, indicating the signal captures only cyclical noise rather than structural margin expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerating below a 2.0 percent annualized pace triggers defensive positioning by institutional investors, leading to equity drawdowns and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below 2.0
failure_condition: The market generates positive 5-day returns in over 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below 2.0, or the signal exhibits zero correlation with subsequent index movements in a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month payroll growth exceeding 150,000 jobs validates underlying economic momentum, reinforcing earnings revision cycles and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns across a full quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 60-day forward returns show no statistical difference between months with payroll growth above 150k and months with growth below 100k over a rolling 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous federal funds rate increases and industrial production declines signal an aggressive tightening cycle outpacing economic activity, which compresses equity valuations and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 40% of historical instances where both series move in the specified directions, or the interaction effect disappears after controlling for volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts contracting for two consecutive months while consumer credit balances stagnate indicate a synchronized pullback in discretionary spending, which reduces corporate top-line guidance and drives negative S&P 500 returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 65% of periods where both housing starts and consumer credit show consecutive monthly declines, proving the signal lacks directional predictive power.
